sourceMovie.title: Flawless
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Flawless for the same reason it broke through in 2007: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Michael Radford keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bank Job
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Flawless clicked for you, The Bank Job (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Flawless?
faq.answer: Try The Bank Job, RocknRolla, Man on a Ledge—each shares DNA with Flawless while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Flawless?
faq.answer: Flawless (2007) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Flawless worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
